{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Partnerships & Growth Lead",
  "description": "At Uwana Energy, we upgrade how you live and work by ensuring you don&#39;t have to think about light or generator issues again! Whether it&#39;s for your comfort at home, productive use, or commercial / industrial purposes, we have a solar system for you. As an indigenous company, we experienced the power challenges and frustrations you go through and we have created a simple path to a better life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Partnerships &amp; Growth Lead

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;(Hybrid)
Job type: Full-time

&nbsp;

Job Summary


	You will own our partnership ecosystem that drives inbound leads while leading the marketing strategy that drives growth across all channels. You will lead our marketing team and work collaboratively with the sales team to ensure deal success.&nbsp;
	We want someone who can negotiate a revenue-share agreement with a microfinance bank partner on Monday, review campaign performance data on Tuesday, and pitch a partnership to a pharmacy chain on Wednesday.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic partnerships:


	Own the full partnership lifecycle: identify, pitch, negotiate, close, and manage agreements&nbsp;
	Structure partnership terms, revenue share, referral fees, co-marketing commitments, and exclusivity where applicable
	Build and manage a pipeline of institutional partners nationwide&nbsp;
	Lead fintech and mobile money integrations (eg. Opay, Moniepoint, PalmPay, MTN) that embed solar financing into their customer journeys
	Develop co-branded programmes with microfinance banks and cooperatives to deliver pre-approved solar loan products to their members


Marketing strategy &amp; oversight:


	Own the overall marketing strategy and budget allocation, working with leadership to set quarterly priorities
	Lead campaign planning for key moments: seasonal pushes, partnership announcements
	Oversee brand positioning and messaging,&nbsp; ensure all external communication reflects the company&#39;s voice and value proposition
	Use performance data to make channel investment decisions
	Define product positioning, value propositions, and messaging that resonate with our target audience.
	Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify growth opportunities and emerging trends.
	Plan and execute PR campaigns, build media relationships, and manage public relations efforts.


Growth &amp; market development:


	Identify and size new market segments, geographies, or customer verticals worth pursuing
	Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders
	Represent the company at industry events, trade fairs, and government forums
	Feed market intelligence back into product and pricing decisions


Requirements
Must-haves:


	4+ years of experience across marketing, partnerships, and/or business development - ideally in solar, energy, fintech, telecoms, or infrastructure
	A track record of closing and managing commercial partnerships or B2B deals with measurable revenue outcomes
	Strong existing network of relevant contacts: corporate procurement teams, fintech business development leads, estate developers, or institutional buyers
	Confident communicator at senior levels, boardrooms, government offices, and community halls alike
	Based in Lagos, with a willingness to travel to other states&nbsp;


Strong advantage if you have:


	Direct experience in the Nigerian solar or off-grid energy sector
	Experience managing a small team or directing external specialists
	Familiarity with CRM tools, pipeline management, and marketing analytics platforms


Compensation
N600,000 / Month&nbsp;",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-25",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-25",

Partnerships & Growth Lead Job at Uwana Energy




        
    

        
        At Uwana Energy, we upgrade how you live and work by ensuring you don't have to think about light or generator issues again! Whether it's for your comfort at home, productive use, or commercial / industrial purposes, we have a solar system for you. As an indigenous company, we experienced the power challenges and frustrations you go through and we have created a simple path to a better life.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Partnerships & Growth Lead

Job Location: Lagos (Hybrid)
Job type: Full-time

 

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	You will own our partnership ecosystem that drives inbound leads while leading the marketing strategy that drives growth across all channels. You will lead our marketing team and work collaboratively with the sales team to ensure deal success. 
	We want someone who can negotiate a revenue-share agreement with a microfinance bank partner on Monday, review campaign performance data on Tuesday, and pitch a partnership to a pharmacy chain on Wednesday.


Key Responsibilities
Strategic partnerships:


	Own the full partnership lifecycle: identify, pitch, negotiate, close, and manage agreements 
	Structure partnership terms, revenue share, referral fees, co-marketing commitments, and exclusivity where applicable
	Build and manage a pipeline of institutional partners nationwide 
	Lead fintech and mobile money integrations (eg. Opay, Moniepoint, PalmPay, MTN) that embed solar financing into their customer journeys
	Develop co-branded programmes with microfinance banks and cooperatives to deliver pre-approved solar loan products to their members


Marketing strategy & oversight:


	Own the overall marketing strategy and budget allocation, working with leadership to set quarterly priorities
	Lead campaign planning for key moments: seasonal pushes, partnership announcements
	Oversee brand positioning and messaging,  ensure all external communication reflects the company's voice and value proposition
	Use performance data to make channel investment decisions
	Define product positioning, value propositions, and messaging that resonate with our target audience.
	Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify growth opportunities and emerging trends.
	Plan and execute PR campaigns, build media relationships, and manage public relations efforts.


Growth & market development:


	Identify and size new market segments, geographies, or customer verticals worth pursuing
	Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders
	Represent the company at industry events, trade fairs, and government forums
	Feed market intelligence back into product and pricing decisions


Requirements
Must-haves:


	4+ years of experience across marketing, partnerships, and/or business development - ideally in solar, energy, fintech, telecoms, or infrastructure
	A track record of closing and managing commercial partnerships or B2B deals with measurable revenue outcomes
	Strong existing network of relevant contacts: corporate procurement teams, fintech business development leads, estate developers, or institutional buyers
	Confident communicator at senior levels, boardrooms, government offices, and community halls alike
	Based in Lagos, with a willingness to travel to other states 


Strong advantage if you have:


	Direct experience in the Nigerian solar or off-grid energy sector
	Experience managing a small team or directing external specialists
	Familiarity with CRM tools, pipeline management, and marketing analytics platforms


Compensation
N600,000 / Month     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Service Center Waterhouse Officer",
  "description": "OPPO, founded in 2004, is one of the world&rsquo;s leading innovators in smart devices and mobile technology. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, Dedicated to delivering cutting-edge innovation, premium user experiences, and stylish designs. OPPO continues to expand its footprint with over 290,000 points of sale and 1,900 official service centers &mdash; bringing the beauty of technology closer to people every day.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Service Center Waterhouse Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Oversee daily stock levels, conduct physical and cycle counts, update inventory records (bin cards, ledgers), and resolve discrepancies.
	Supervise incoming deliveries, verify quality and quantity against orders, prepare goods for storage and shipment, and ensure accurate dispatch.
	Maintain proper warehouse layout, ensure equipment is functional, liaise with sales, logistics, and suppliers, and provide regular status reports.
	Inspect goods for damage, ensure compliance with safety standards, and maintain a clean and organized warehouse.
	Support the Warehouse Manager in supervising warehouse staff and enforcing standard operating procedures.


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of a High School Diploma with relevant experience in stock or warehouse management with&nbsp;1-2 years experience.
	Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in computer usage, MS Office (Excel, Word), and inventory management software.
	Ability to multitask, work under pressure, and operate in a physically demanding environment.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;cvpool2021@oppo-aed.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Service Center Waterhouse Officer Job at OPPO




        
    

        
        OPPO, founded in 2004, is one of the world’s leading innovators in smart devices and mobile technology. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, Dedicated to delivering cutting-edge innovation, premium user experiences, and stylish designs. OPPO continues to expand its footprint with over 290,000 points of sale and 1,900 official service centers — bringing the beauty of technology closer to people every day.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Service Center Waterhouse Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee daily stock levels, conduct physical and cycle counts, update inventory records (bin cards, ledgers), and resolve discrepancies.
	Supervise incoming deliveries, verify quality and quantity against orders, prepare goods for storage and shipment, and ensure accurate dispatch.
	Maintain proper warehouse layout, ensure equipment is functional, liaise with sales, logistics, and suppliers, and provide regular status reports.
	Inspect goods for damage, ensure compliance with safety standards, and maintain a clean and organized warehouse.
	Support the Warehouse Manager in supervising warehouse staff and enforcing standard operating procedures.


Required Skills & Qualifications


	Minimum of a High School Diploma with relevant experience in stock or warehouse management with 1-2 years experience.
	Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficiency in computer usage, MS Office (Excel, Word), and inventory management software.
	Ability to multitask, work under pressure, and operate in a physically demanding environment.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Area Sales Manager (ASM)",
  "description": "Sonia Foods Industries is one of the leading tomato products processing factories in West Africa. Its headquarters is located on the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway, KM 39. The company produces Tomato Mix, Peppe &amp; Onion Tomato Seasoning Mix, Curry Powder, and Thyme Leaves, under the brand name Sonia. The factory is located on Ikorodu Shagamu Express Road, Onigbagbe/Sotubo Village, Shagamu, Ogun State, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Area Sales Manager (ASM)

Job Locations:&nbsp;Benin - Edo, Ilorin- Kwara, Akure - Ondo, Osogbo - Osun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Area Sales Manager (ASM) is responsible for driving sales growth, market expansion, distributor performance, and execution excellence within the assigned territory.
	The role ensures effective implementation of company sales strategies, achievement of volume and value targets, and strong team leadership to deliver sustainable business results.


Key Responsibilities
Sales &amp; Business Performance:


	Develop and execute area sales plans in line with company objectives and annual targets.
	Drive achievement of sales volume, value, distribution, and market share targets.
	Monitor sales performance trends and take corrective actions where required.
	Ensure effective coverage, route-to-market efficiency, and outlet expansion within the territory.
	Manage trade promotions, pricing compliance, and product availability.


Distributor &amp; Channel Management:


	Manage and strengthen distributor relationships to ensure optimal service levels and stock availability.
	Review distributor KPIs, including sales growth, stock rotation, credit management, and operational efficiency.
	Ensure timely order processing, stock replenishment, and adherence to agreed commercial terms.
	Identify and develop new distributors or sub-distributors where necessary.


Team Leadership &amp; Capability Development:


	Lead, coach, and motivate Sales Supervisors and Sales Representatives within the area.
	Conduct regular field coaching, joint calls, and performance reviews.
	Ensure proper deployment, productivity, and discipline of the sales force.
	Identify training needs and support continuous capability development.


Execution Excellence &amp; Market Intelligence:


	Ensure excellent in-store execution, visibility, merchandising standards, and promotional compliance.
	Monitor competitor activities, pricing, and market dynamics, providing timely market intelligence.
	Support new product launches and ensure effective market penetration.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, SOPs, and ethical standards.


Reporting &amp; Administration:


	Prepare and submit accurate daily, weekly, and monthly sales reports.
	Track area performance using approved reporting tools and dashboards.
	Manage area budgets and ensure cost-effective operations.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Achievement of sales volume and value targets.
	Distributor performance and coverage expansion.
	Sales force productivity and capability development.
	Trade execution and market visibility standards.
	Accuracy and timeliness of reporting.


Qualifications &amp; Experience
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or a related discipline.
	An MBA or relevant professional certification is an added advantage.


Experience Requirements:


	Minimum of 8 years total sales experience in the FMCG industry.
	At least 4 years of FMCG experience with a reputable organization.
	Minimum of 3 years of proven experience as an Area Sales Manager (ASM).
	Strong background progressing from Salesman/Sales Representative to leadership roles.


Key Competencies &amp; Skills:


	Strong leadership, people management, and coaching skills.
	Excellent knowledge of FMCG route-to-market operations.
	Sound analytical, planning, and problem-solving abilities.
	Strong negotiation and distributor management skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and result orientation.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and sales reporting tools.
	Willingness to travel extensively within the assigned area.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send&nbsp;their CV to:&nbsp;careers@blumedistribution.com&nbsp;and copy&nbsp;hr@blumedistribution.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

        Sonia Foods Industries is one of the leading tomato products processing factories in West Africa. Its headquarters is located on the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway, KM 39. The company produces Tomato Mix, Peppe & Onion Tomato Seasoning Mix, Curry Powder, and Thyme Leaves, under the brand name Sonia. The factory is located on Ikorodu Shagamu Express Road, Onigbagbe/Sotubo Village, Shagamu, Ogun State, Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Area Sales Manager (ASM)

Job Locations: Benin - Edo, Ilorin- Kwara, Akure - Ondo, Osogbo - Osun
Employment Type: Full-time

	The Area Sales Manager (ASM) is responsible for driving sales growth, market expansion, distributor performance, and execution excellence within the assigned territory.

	Identify training needs and support continuous capability development.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Cybersecurity Officer",
  "description": "We are a leading oil and gas company operating in Nigeria&rsquo;s downstream sector with head office in Port Harcourt, Rivers State.
To meet the growing demand of Nigeria&rsquo;s Market, Stockgap Fuels Limited is expanding its LPG business by investing on additional storage and cylinder filling facility in 4 strategic locations spanning major regions of the country - Lagos/South-West, South-South/South-East, North-Central/North-West and North-East regions respectively.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cybersecurity Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Cybersecurity Officer is responsible for protecting the organization&rsquo;s information systems, networks, and data from cyber threats.
	This role focuses on threat detection, prevention, and response, ensuring that all digital assets&mdash;including enterprise applications, cloud platforms, and user endpoints&mdash;are secure and compliant with industry standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor networks, systems, and applications for security threats
	Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing
	Track and manage remediation of identified risks and ensure timely patching of systems and applications
	Detect and respond to cybersecurity incidents (e.g., malware, phishing, breaches)
	Implement and manage security tools (firewalls, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection)
	Ensure secure configurations of systems and applications
	Support secure design of new systems and integrations
	Enforce access control policies (least privilege, role-based access)
	Ensure data encryption (at rest and in transit)
	Implement data loss prevention (DLP) strategies
	Manage backups and disaster recovery security
	Ensure compliance with security standards and regulations
	Educate staff on cybersecurity best practices
	Conduct phishing simulations and awareness campaigns


Skills and Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Computer Science
	3&ndash;5+ years in cybersecurity or information security roles
	Experience in security monitoring, incident response, or risk management


Technical Skills:


	Security Tools
	SIEM tools (e.g., Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel)
	Firewalls and intrusion detection/prevention systems
	Endpoint security tools (EDR/XDR)
	Cloud security (Azure, AWS)
	Network security (VPNs, segmentation)
	Certifications (Preferred)
	CISM or CISA


Core Competencies:


	Protection of operational systems (e.g., depot systems, logistics platforms)
	Security of ERP platforms (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics)
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Attention to detail and quality
	Ability to work independently and in teams
	Good communication and stakeholder management skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and cover letter to:&nbsp;careers@stockgapfuels.com&nbsp;using thhe Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Cybersecurity Officer Job at Stockgap Fuels Limited




        
    

        
        We are a leading oil and gas company operating in Nigeria’s downstream sector with head office in Port Harcourt, Rivers State.
To meet the growing demand of Nigeria’s Market, Stockgap Fuels Limited is expanding its LPG business by investing on additional storage and cylinder filling facility in 4 strategic locations spanning major regions of the country - Lagos/South-West, South-South/South-East, North-Central/North-West and North-East regions respectively.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Cybersecurity Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Cybersecurity Officer is responsible for protecting the organization’s information systems, networks, and data from cyber threats.
	This role focuses on threat detection, prevention, and response, ensuring that all digital assets—including enterprise applications, cloud platforms, and user endpoints—are secure and compliant with industry standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor networks, systems, and applications for security threats
	Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and penetration testing
	Track and manage remediation of identified risks and ensure timely patching of systems and applications
	Detect and respond to cybersecurity incidents (e.g., malware, phishing, breaches)
	Implement and manage security tools (firewalls, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection)
	Ensure secure configurations of systems and applications
	Support secure design of new systems and integrations
	Enforce access control policies (least privilege, role-based access)
	Ensure data encryption (at rest and in transit)
	Implement data loss prevention (DLP) strategies
	Manage backups and disaster recovery security
	Ensure compliance with security standards and regulations
	Educate staff on cybersecurity best practices
	Conduct phishing simulations and awareness campaigns


Skills and Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Computer Science
	3–5+ years in cybersecurity or information security roles
	Experience in security monitoring, incident response, or risk management


Technical Skills:


	Security Tools
	SIEM tools (e.g., Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel)
	Firewalls and intrusion detection/prevention systems
	Endpoint security tools (EDR/XDR)
	Cloud security (Azure, AWS)
	Network security (VPNs, segmentation)
	Certifications (Preferred)
	CISM or CISA


Core Competencies:


	Protection of operational systems (e.g., depot systems, logistics platforms)
	Security of ERP platforms (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics)
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Attention to detail and quality
	Ability to work independently and in teams
	Good communication and stakeholder management skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using thhe Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Fiber Engineer Job at Dotmac Technologies Limited




        
    

        
        Dotmac Technologies Limited - We are an Information & Communications Technology Solutions Company formed in 2008, located in Abuja, Nigeria. We deliver reliable, high performance IT Solutions and services to corporate, business and residential clients. We offer solutions that improve your operation, optimizing and delivering efficiency.

We offer a wide range of Services that are designed to improve and optimize the services of our clients. These include Network services, IT Security Solutions and cloud services. In meeting our clients' needs, Dotmac designs solutions and integrates the requisite systems to achieve a pre agreed objective.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fiber Engineer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Contract

Requirements


	OND and HND students needing industrial training or internship placement
	SIWES applicants in engineering, computer science, or related fields
	Anyone with genuine interest in fiber, wireless, or network engineering
	Candidates who can commit to the full program schedule
	NYSC corp members looking for PPA placement in a technical role


Selection criteria


	Genuine interest in telecom, networking, or ISP operations
	Willingness to learn and follow instructions in a structured environment
	Strong attitude, discipline, and consistency
	Availability for the internship period in Abuja or Lagos
	Basic computer literacy and willingness to work in the field
